title: rhinanthus serotinus subsp. aestivalis
intro: Rhinanthus serotinus subsp. aestivalis, also known as the late summer or summer-fruited yellow rattle, is a subspecies of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae. This parasitic plant has been used in herbal medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects. The health benefits of Rhinanthus serotinus subsp. aestivalis include reducing inflammation, improving cardiovascular health, and supporting the immune system. It can be prepared as a herbal tea, tincture, or infusion, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
